goal: v0.2: drop deprecated reconcile alias entirely; sync auto-pins after rebuild so users never need to invoke pin separately.

summary.title: v0.2: drop reconcile alias, sync auto-pins, manual pin <intent> <commit> stays as fallback
summary.what:
Two coordinated v0.2 cleanups in one branch. (1) Removes the deprecated `mainline reconcile` CLI command and the corresponding Service.Reconcile / ReconcileManual / ReconcileResult / ReconciledLink Go surfaces. The on-wire `via=reconcile_*` values continue to render correctly through sync.normaliseVia — git-notes history is immutable, the bridge stays. (2) Adds AutoPinAfterSync (default true, backfilled like the rc5 fields) so Service.Sync runs the pin strategy cascade between rebuildView and AutoCheckAfterSync, then re-rebuilds to surface post-pin merged statuses to the rest of sync's outputs. CLI exposes the result as SyncResult.AutoPinned and prints per-link triples in non-JSON output. The user-facing pin command becomes manual-only — cobra ExactArgs(2) so `mainline pin` (no args) errors out with a helpful 'requires 2 args' message; the no-args batch variant is gone because sync covers that.

Notably preserved: Service.Pin (no-arg) is internal — Service.Sync calls it. Service.PinExplicit serves the manual fallback CLI. The PinResult / PinnedCommit types stay; the 4 tests that asserted on svc.Reconcile().Reconciled were rewired to assert on syncRes.AutoPinned (now the natural assertion target). One test (TestReconcileDoesNotEvictExistingIntent) had to switch to --offline seal so the explicit Sync is what triggers the upsert path it tests, not SealSubmit's internal sync.
summary.why: User pushed back on the original 'kill all of pin including the manual variant' direction. The right reading: pin is a *system* concern, not a user concern, but the manual escape hatch (heavily rebased history, cherry-picks across forks, CI/automation, reviewer pinning someone else's intent) makes the two-arg form load-bearing. Removing it would force users into raw `git notes ... add` for any case the heuristic cascade misses. Removing only the no-args batch variant honours the principle — daily users see pin only as 'an internal step inside sync' — without amputating the fallback. Same logic applied to reconcile: the alias was sentimental, the real surface is pin, and one minor-version of advance notice is enough.
summary.user_goal: Make sync the single command that drives the team-aware loop, while keeping a small, documented escape hatch for the cases the heuristic cannot cover.

decisions.rationale: Sync now does what no-args pin used to. Two commands doing the same thing in the same loop is confusing; making the no-args form an error with a Usage line teaches users to call sync instead. The two-arg form is unambiguously the manual fallback.
rejected: Keep no-args as 'rare debug' — invites users to think pin is something they should run; no-args has no real-world non-debug case left.
Hide pin entirely behind --advanced — too aggressive; CI / reviewer help paths still need the two-arg form.

decisions.chose: Yes — pin is now manual-fallback only; the user already knows the commit they want to pin
decisions.rationale: Auto-syncing before a manual pin would not change the pin's outcome — the (intent, commit) tuple is given. Saves an SSH round trip on every fallback invocation.

decisions.chose: Yes — second rebuildView call after Pin() returns Pinned > 0
decisions.rationale: Without it, the post-pin view still reports the pinned intents as proposed, the proposed_index is stale, and AutoCheckAfterSync runs against the wrong status set. The cost is one extra rebuild (cheap — local file I/O, no network). Pin's local NotesAdd has already updated the notes ref so the second rebuild picks them up.
rejected: In-place patch the view's Status field for each pinned intent — works but duplicates rebuildView's logic; small performance win, large correctness risk.

decisions.chose: Make the second seal --offline so the test's explicit Sync is the one that triggers the upsert
decisions.rationale: The test verifies an upsert invariant (one commit can carry two intents in its note). With auto-pin in seal's internal sync, the upsert was already happening before the test's explicit Sync, leaving Sync with nothing to pin. --offline seal preserves the test's intent — the upsert behaviour is still tested, just at a different step in the pipeline.

risks: Existing scripts calling `mainline reconcile` now hard-fail with 'unknown command'. Acceptable: rc4 already shipped a deprecation warning pointing at `pin`; v0.2 was the announced removal window.
The re-rebuild after auto-pin adds 50-200 ms per sync (rebuilds parse all events). Imperceptible interactively but measurable in tight loops; can cache the prior view if it ever matters.

TestReconcileDoesNotEvictExistingIntent now relies on --offline preserving SealSubmit's pre-sync state. If --offline ever stops being a real bypass (e.g. someone wires sync into a different lifecycle hook), this test will silently start passing for the wrong reason.
followups: Track how often the manual `mainline pin <intent> <commit>` form is invoked — if dogfood data shows < 1 use per week per team, consider hiding it behind a --manual flag for further surface reduction.
v0.3 / GitHub Action milestone: the manual pin form is exactly the call shape a post-merge Action wants — `mainline pin $INTENT_ID $MERGE_SHA`.
Add a `--no-pin` flag to sync so power users / debuggers can skip auto-pin in a particular sync.

architectural_claims: Sync is the single command that drives the team-aware loop (fetch + rebuild + auto-pin + auto-check + write LastSync).
User-surface pin is manual fallback only; auto-pin lives inside Sync.
Wire-format reconcile_* via values continue to be parseable but no new write produces them.
behavioral_changes: mainline pin without arguments errors; only mainline pin <intent> <commit> is accepted.
mainline sync auto-pins after rebuild and prints 'Auto-pinned N intent(s)' when matches happen.
SyncResult JSON gains an auto_pinned[] array.

api_changes.signature: mainline pin now requires 2 args (was 0 or 2)

migration_notes: Scripts / Makefiles that invoked `mainline reconcile` must change to `mainline pin <intent> <commit>` (manual fallback) or just rely on `mainline sync`.
Scripts that invoked `mainline pin` with no args must change to `mainline sync` — the auto-pin behaviour is now a sync step.
